lexemes.definition: A polite phrase used when you meet someone for the first time.
collocations: It is nice to meet you

lexemes.definition: The be-verb form used with I.
A person who studies in the same class as you.
A word used to say a person's origin or starting place.
To use a language when talking.
A word for one thing near the speaker.
The people closely related to you.
A usual set of actions you do regularly.
A word meaning that something happens most of the time.
lexemes.exampleSentence: I usually eat breakfast at seven.

lexemes.definition: Close to a place or thing.
lexemes.exampleSentence: The cafe is near the station.

lexemes.definition: To ask for food or drink in a cafe or restaurant.
lexemes.exampleSentence: I would like to order a coffee.
lexemes.translation: ask to buy food or drink

lexemes.definition: How large or small clothes or objects are.
A word used to talk about ability or possibility.
To ask someone to come to an event or activity.
lexemes.exampleSentence: I want to invite Mia to the party.

lexemes.definition: A phrase used to say that something is necessary.
lexemes.exampleSentence: I have to finish my homework.

lexemes.definition: The day before today.
An arrangement to have a hotel room, seat, or service.
lexemes.exampleSentence: I have a booking for two nights.

lexemes.definition: An opinion or suggestion about what someone should do.
lexemes.exampleSentence: The doctor gave me useful advice.

lexemes.definition: To look at two or more things and say how they are similar or different.
Something that happened to you or that you have done.
lexemes.exampleSentence: Have you ever had this experience?
lexemes.translation: thing you have done or lived through

units.title: Starting Conversations
units.focus: Greetings, be, names, countries, classroom basics, family words

lessons.title: Hello There
lessons.subtitle: Greetings and names
lessons.goal: Greet someone, give your name, and reply politely.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Greet someone, give your name, and reply politely.
tips: Use I am for a full self-introduction.
Nice to meet you is the usual first reply.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best reply.
exercises.promptDetail: A: Hi, I am Yuki. ____
exercises.hint: Think about what people say after an introduction.
exercises.explanation: Nice to meet you is the natural reply when someone tells you their name.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: Hello, I ___ Ken.
exercises.hint: Use the verb be with I.
exercises.explanation: Use am after I in a basic introduction.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
exercises.hint: Start with the subject, then the verb.
exercises.explanation: The natural order is subject, verb, then the noun phrase.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: My name are Aiko
exercises.hint: Check the verb after my name.
exercises.explanation: This sentence is not correct. Say My name is Aiko.

lessons.title: Where Are You From?
lessons.subtitle: Countries and languages
lessons.goal: Say where people are from and what languages they speak.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Say where people are from and what languages they speak.
tips: Use from before a country name.
Use speak with languages such as English or Spanish.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best answer.
exercises.promptDetail: A: Where are you from? B: ____
exercises.hint: Answer with a country.
exercises.explanation: I am from Brazil directly answers a question about origin.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: Luis is from Spain. He speaks ___.
exercises.hint: Use the language, not the country.
exercises.explanation: Spanish is the language spoken in Spain.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
exercises.hint: Keep be before from Canada.
exercises.explanation: Use subject plus be, then from and the country name.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: Emma from Australia
exercises.hint: A full sentence needs a verb.
exercises.explanation: This sentence is not correct. Say Emma is from Australia.

lessons.title: In the Classroom
lessons.subtitle: This, that, and school objects
lessons.goal: Identify classroom objects and ask simple questions.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Identify classroom objects and ask simple questions.
tips: Use this for something near you.
Use an before a vowel sound such as eraser.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best question.
exercises.promptDetail: You point to a book in your hand. ____
exercises.hint: You are asking about an object near you.
exercises.explanation: What is this is the natural question for an object close to you.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: That is ___ eraser on the desk.
exercises.hint: Eraser begins with a vowel sound.
exercises.explanation: Use an before eraser because it starts with a vowel sound.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
exercises.hint: Begin with This is.
exercises.explanation: This is our English teacher is a clear way to introduce a person nearby.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: These is my notebooks
exercises.hint: Check the verb with these.
exercises.explanation: This sentence is not correct. Say These are my notebooks.

lessons.title: Meet My Family
lessons.subtitle: Family words and ages
lessons.goal: Talk about family members, ages, and possession.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Talk about family members, ages, and possession.
tips: Use my, his, or her before family words.
Use has with he, she, or it.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: You talk about your brother.
exercises.hint: Use is with he.
exercises.explanation: He is twenty years old is the correct pattern for age.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: Mika has two sisters, and ___ father is a doctor.
exercises.hint: The father belongs to Mika.
exercises.explanation: Use her because the sentence is about Mika and her father.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
exercises.hint: Place the verb after the subject.
exercises.explanation: My parents live in Osaka is a simple, natural family sentence.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: Our grandmother has 70 years old
exercises.hint: Check the pattern for age.
exercises.explanation: This sentence is not correct. Say Our grandmother is 70 years old.

units.title: Daily Rhythm
units.focus: Present simple, time, routines, adverbs of frequency, weekend activities

lessons.title: Morning Routine
lessons.subtitle: Simple daily actions
lessons.goal: Describe simple morning routines with present simple verbs.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Describe simple morning routines with present simple verbs.
tips: Use base verbs after I, you, we, and they.
Time words such as every morning help make routines clear.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Every weekday, Hana gets up at 6:30 and eats toast at home.
choices: She goes to bed at five
exercises.hint: Match the sentence to the routine.
exercises.explanation: She has breakfast at home matches the information in the routine.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: I brush my teeth before I ___ to work.
exercises.hint: Use the present simple base verb after I.
exercises.explanation: Go completes the present simple sentence correctly.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: She takes the bus every morning
exercises.hint: Put the time phrase at the end.
exercises.explanation: The natural order is subject, verb, object, then the time phrase.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: He eat breakfast at seven
exercises.hint: Check the verb with he.
exercises.explanation: This sentence is not correct. Say He eats breakfast at seven.

lessons.title: What Time Is It?
lessons.subtitle: Clock times and schedules
lessons.goal: Tell the time and ask about simple schedules.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Tell the time and ask about simple schedules.
tips: Use at with clock times.
Half past ten means 10:30.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best answer.
exercises.promptDetail: A: What time is your class? B: ____
exercises.hint: Answer with a time expression.
exercises.explanation: It starts at nine thirty directly answers a question about schedule.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: The movie starts ___ 7:15.
exercises.hint: Use the usual preposition for clock times.
exercises.explanation: Use at before an exact time such as 7:15.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: My train leaves at eight forty
exercises.hint: Keep the time phrase together after leaves.
exercises.explanation: My train leaves at eight forty is the standard word order.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: The phrase half past ten means 10:30
exercises.hint: Think about half an hour after ten.
exercises.explanation: Half past ten is another way to say 10:30.

lessons.title: Busy Weekdays
lessons.subtitle: Adverbs of frequency
lessons.goal: Use adverbs of frequency to describe weekday habits.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Use adverbs of frequency to describe weekday habits.
tips: Put adverbs like often and sometimes before the main verb.
Use always to describe a very regular habit.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Sara goes running on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday.
exercises.hint: Think about a habit that happens many times.
exercises.explanation: Often fits a habit that happens several times each week.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: I ___ check my email before breakfast.
exercises.hint: Use an adverb for a very regular action.
exercises.explanation: Always works because the action happens every day in this routine.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: They sometimes study at the library
exercises.hint: Put sometimes before the main verb.
exercises.explanation: They sometimes study at the library uses natural adverb placement.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: He always is late
exercises.hint: Check where always usually goes.
exercises.explanation: This sentence sounds unnatural here. Say He is always late or He is often late.

lessons.title: Weekend Plans
lessons.subtitle: Free time activities
lessons.goal: Talk about weekend activities and simple preferences.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Talk about weekend activities and simple preferences.
tips: Use do for routine questions such as What do you do on Sundays?
Use likes with he or she.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best reply.
exercises.promptDetail: A: What do you do on Sundays? B: ____
exercises.hint: Answer with a usual weekend activity.
exercises.explanation: I visit my grandparents is a natural reply about a regular Sunday activity.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: My friends and I ___ soccer in the park on Saturdays.
exercises.hint: Use the main action verb.
exercises.explanation: Play completes the present simple sentence correctly.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: She likes watching movies on weekends
exercises.hint: Keep likes before the activity.
exercises.explanation: She likes watching movies on weekends is the natural word order.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: We goes hiking on Sunday
exercises.hint: Check the verb after we.
exercises.explanation: This sentence is not correct. Say We go hiking on Sunday.

units.title: Home and Neighborhood
units.focus: There is and there are, place words, prepositions, directions

lessons.title: My Apartment
lessons.subtitle: Rooms and furniture
lessons.goal: Describe rooms and furniture with there is and there are.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Describe rooms and furniture with there is and there are.
tips: Use there is for one thing.
Use there are for plural nouns.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Your living room has one sofa and two lamps.
choices: There is a sofa and two lamps
There are a sofa and two lamps
acceptableAnswers: There is a sofa and two lamps
exercises.hint: Start with the single item first.
exercises.explanation: There is a sofa and two lamps works because the sentence begins with the singular item.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: There ___ a small desk in my bedroom.
exercises.hint: Desk is singular.
exercises.explanation: Use is with the singular noun desk.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: There are three chairs in the kitchen
exercises.hint: Use there are with a plural noun.
exercises.explanation: There are three chairs in the kitchen follows the standard pattern.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: There are a bathroom upstairs
exercises.hint: Bathroom is singular.
exercises.explanation: This sentence is not correct. Say There is a bathroom upstairs.

lessons.title: Around My Town
lessons.subtitle: Town places
lessons.goal: Say where common places in town are located.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Say where common places in town are located.
tips: Near, next to, and between help you place buildings.
A location answer often uses is plus a place phrase.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: You want to buy medicine.
choices: The pharmacy is next to the bank
The bakery is under my bed
acceptableAnswers: The pharmacy is next to the bank
exercises.hint: Pick the sentence about a real town place.
exercises.explanation: The pharmacy is next to the bank is a natural location sentence for town navigation.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: The library is ___ the post office and the cafe.
exercises.hint: The library has one place on each side.
exercises.explanation: Between is the correct preposition when something is in the middle of two places.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: Our school is near the river
exercises.hint: Place near the river after the verb.
exercises.explanation: Our school is near the river is the natural sentence order.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: The supermarket is on Green Street
exercises.hint: This is a normal way to answer a location question.
exercises.explanation: The supermarket is on Green Street is a correct and useful location sentence.

lessons.title: Where Is the Cafe?
lessons.subtitle: Prepositions of place
lessons.goal: Use common prepositions to describe where places are.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Use common prepositions to describe where places are.
tips: Across from shows the opposite side.
In front of needs two words after in.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best answer.
exercises.promptDetail: A: Where is the cafe? B: It is ____ the bookstore.
exercises.hint: You need a place preposition.
exercises.explanation: Across from is a place expression that fits the question.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: The bus stop is ___ front of the station.
exercises.hint: Use the fixed phrase in front of.
exercises.explanation: Use in to complete the phrase in front of.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: The hotel is behind the museum
exercises.hint: Keep behind before the place.
exercises.explanation: The hotel is behind the museum is the correct word order.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: The park is between the school
exercises.hint: Between usually needs two places.
exercises.explanation: This sentence is not complete. Between usually needs two points, such as between the school and the bank.

lessons.title: Directions on Foot
lessons.subtitle: Basic street directions
lessons.goal: Ask for and give simple walking directions.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Ask for and give simple walking directions.
tips: Use turn left and turn right for corners.
Go straight is useful before the next landmark.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best instruction.
exercises.promptDetail: You want someone to move left at the corner.
exercises.hint: Choose the sentence that gives a clear direction.
exercises.explanation: Turn left at the corner is the direct instruction for this situation.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: Go straight, and then ___ right at the traffic light.
exercises.hint: Use another direction verb.
exercises.explanation: Turn completes the instruction naturally.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: Walk past the bank and stop at the bridge
exercises.hint: Put the first action first.
exercises.explanation: Walk past the bank and stop at the bridge is a natural sequence of directions.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: Go straight for two blocks
exercises.hint: This is a standard street instruction.
exercises.explanation: Go straight for two blocks is a correct and useful direction sentence.

units.title: Food and Shopping
units.focus: Food preferences, polite orders, countable and uncountable nouns, prices and size

lessons.title: Breakfast at Home
lessons.subtitle: Food likes and simple meals
lessons.goal: Talk about breakfast foods and basic preferences.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Talk about breakfast foods and basic preferences.
tips: Use like and do not like for personal taste.
Some works well with plural or uncountable food words in positive sentences.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Yuki eats eggs every morning, but she does not drink coffee.
choices: She likes eggs but not coffee
She likes coffee but not eggs
acceptableAnswers: She likes eggs but not coffee
exercises.hint: Match both parts of the information.
exercises.explanation: She likes eggs but not coffee matches the two preferences in the prompt.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: We have ___ bread and fruit for breakfast.
exercises.hint: Use a word that works with bread and fruit in a positive sentence.
exercises.explanation: Some fits naturally with bread and fruit in a positive statement.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: I usually drink tea with breakfast
exercises.hint: Put usually before the main verb.
exercises.explanation: I usually drink tea with breakfast uses natural adverb placement.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: I do not like milk
exercises.hint: This sentence states a preference clearly.
exercises.explanation: I do not like milk is a correct way to talk about food preference.

lessons.title: At the Cafe
lessons.subtitle: Polite food orders
lessons.goal: Order simple food and drinks politely.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Order simple food and drinks politely.
tips: Would like sounds more polite than want in a cafe.
Use a before a singular countable drink or dish.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best order.
exercises.promptDetail: You want one sandwich and one orange juice.
choices: I would like a sandwich and an orange juice
acceptableAnswers: I would like a sandwich and an orange juice
exercises.hint: Pick the polite full sentence.
exercises.explanation: I would like a sandwich and an orange juice is a clear and polite cafe order.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: Could I have ___ cup of tea, please?
exercises.hint: Cup is singular and countable.
exercises.explanation: Use a before the singular noun phrase cup of tea.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: Can we see the menu please
exercises.hint: Start with Can we.
exercises.explanation: Can we see the menu please is a polite question for cafe service.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: I would like two coffees
exercises.hint: This is a normal polite order.
exercises.explanation: I would like two coffees is correct when ordering two cups of coffee.

lessons.title: In the Supermarket
lessons.subtitle: Some, any, and food shopping
lessons.goal: Use countable and uncountable nouns when shopping for food.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Use countable and uncountable nouns when shopping for food.
tips: Use any in many negative sentences and questions.
Use much with uncountable nouns such as rice or milk.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Your fridge is empty.
choices: We do not have any milk
We do not have many bread
acceptableAnswers: We do not have any milk
exercises.hint: Milk is uncountable and the sentence is negative.
exercises.explanation: We do not have any milk is the correct pattern for an uncountable noun in a negative sentence.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: There are ___ apples in the basket.
exercises.hint: Use a positive quantity word with apples.
exercises.explanation: Some is the natural choice in a positive sentence with plural apples.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: Do you need any rice today
exercises.hint: Use any in this question.
exercises.explanation: Do you need any rice today is a correct shopping question.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: How much bananas do we need
exercises.hint: Bananas are countable and plural.
exercises.explanation: This sentence is not correct. Say How many bananas do we need.

lessons.title: Trying on Clothes
lessons.subtitle: Size, color, and price
lessons.goal: Ask about clothes, size, and price in a shop.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Ask about clothes, size, and price in a shop.
tips: Use How much is for price.
Use larger or smaller when you compare sizes.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best question.
exercises.promptDetail: You want to know the cost of a jacket.
exercises.hint: Choose the sentence about price.
exercises.explanation: How much is this jacket is the standard way to ask the price of one item.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: These shoes are too small. Do you have them in a larger ___?
exercises.hint: You need the usual shopping word for fit.
exercises.explanation: Size completes the sentence naturally in a clothes shop.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: This shirt is cheaper than that one
exercises.hint: Put cheaper before than that one.
exercises.explanation: This shirt is cheaper than that one is the correct comparative pattern.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: I am looking for a black coat
exercises.hint: This is a useful shopping sentence.
exercises.explanation: I am looking for a black coat is a clear way to ask for help in a shop.

units.title: Friends and Free Time
units.focus: Hobbies, can and cannot, invitations, timetables for activities

lessons.title: Hobbies and Interests
lessons.subtitle: Likes and favorite activities
lessons.goal: Say what people like doing in their free time.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Say what people like doing in their free time.
tips: Use like, love, or enjoy before activities.
A verb after enjoy usually ends in ing.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Omar enjoys drawing and cooking after work.
choices: He is cooking every winter in Canada
exercises.hint: Pick the sentence about free-time interests.
exercises.explanation: He likes drawing and cooking matches the hobbies in the prompt.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: My sister loves ___ photos in the park.
exercises.hint: Use the ing form after loves here.
exercises.explanation: Taking works naturally after loves in this sentence.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: They enjoy playing board games together
exercises.hint: Keep playing with the activity.
exercises.explanation: They enjoy playing board games together is the natural word order.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: She likes read novels
exercises.hint: Check the form after likes.
exercises.explanation: This sentence is not correct. Say She likes reading novels or She likes to read novels.

lessons.title: What Can You Do?
lessons.subtitle: Ability and help
lessons.goal: Talk about ability with can and cannot.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Talk about ability with can and cannot.
tips: Use the base verb after can.
Cannot can describe lack of ability or an unavailable action.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Mina can swim, but she cannot drive.
choices: She can swim but she cannot drive
She can drives but cannot swim
acceptableAnswers: She can swim but she cannot drive
exercises.hint: Use the base verb after can and cannot.
exercises.explanation: She can swim but she cannot drive uses the correct can pattern.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: Can your brother ___ the guitar?
exercises.hint: Use the base verb after can.
exercises.explanation: Play is the correct verb form after can.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: We cannot come to practice today
exercises.hint: Keep cannot before the main verb.
exercises.explanation: We cannot come to practice today is a natural ability or availability sentence.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: Can you help me carry this box
exercises.hint: This asks for help politely.
exercises.explanation: Can you help me carry this box is a correct and useful request.

lessons.title: Making Plans
lessons.subtitle: Invitations and meeting up
lessons.goal: Invite someone and respond to simple plans.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Invite someone and respond to simple plans.
tips: Would you like to is a polite invitation.
Use meet with a place or time to make plans clear.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best reply.
exercises.promptDetail: A: Would you like to watch a movie on Friday? B: ____
exercises.hint: Reply to the invitation directly.
exercises.explanation: Yes, that sounds great is a natural positive reply to an invitation.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: Let us ___ at the station at six.
exercises.hint: Use the verb for coming together.
exercises.explanation: Meet completes the plan naturally.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: Do you want to play tennis after class
exercises.hint: Start with the invitation question.
exercises.explanation: Do you want to play tennis after class is a clear invitation.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: How about Sunday afternoon
exercises.hint: This is a common way to suggest a time.
exercises.explanation: How about Sunday afternoon is a correct and useful suggestion.

lessons.title: At the Sports Center
lessons.subtitle: Class times and equipment
lessons.goal: Talk about activity schedules and what you need.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Talk about activity schedules and what you need.
tips: Use opens, starts, and ends for schedules.
Use for before the activity when you name needed items.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: The yoga class is on Tuesday and Thursday evenings.
choices: The yoga class is twice a week
The yoga class is in the kitchen
The yoga class is my cousin
acceptableAnswers: The yoga class is twice a week
exercises.hint: Count how often the class happens.
exercises.explanation: The yoga class is twice a week correctly summarizes the schedule.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: You need sports shoes ___ the gym class.
exercises.hint: Use the preposition that shows purpose.
exercises.explanation: For is the natural preposition before the activity.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: The pool opens at seven in the morning
exercises.hint: Put the time phrase after opens.
exercises.explanation: The pool opens at seven in the morning is the correct schedule sentence.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: Bring a towel and a water bottle
exercises.hint: This gives useful class information.
exercises.explanation: Bring a towel and a water bottle is a correct instruction for a sports class.

units.title: Work and Study
units.focus: School schedules, jobs, present continuous, obligations with have to

lessons.title: At School
lessons.subtitle: Subjects and opinions
lessons.goal: Describe school subjects and simple class schedules.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Describe school subjects and simple class schedules.
tips: Use favorite plus subject to talk about what you enjoy.
School schedules often use on plus the day.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Aiko has math on Monday and English on Wednesday.
exercises.hint: Match the day and subject correctly.
exercises.explanation: She has English on Wednesday matches the schedule in the prompt.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: My favorite subject ___ history because the stories are interesting.
exercises.hint: Subject is singular.
exercises.explanation: Use is with the singular noun phrase my favorite subject.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: We have science after lunch on Tuesday
exercises.hint: Put the time phrases after the subject and verb.
exercises.explanation: We have science after lunch on Tuesday is the natural schedule order.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: Our homework are difficult today
exercises.hint: Homework is treated as singular here.
exercises.explanation: This sentence is not correct. Say Our homework is difficult today.

lessons.title: At Work
lessons.subtitle: Jobs and workplace tasks
lessons.goal: Talk about jobs and everyday tasks at work.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Talk about jobs and everyday tasks at work.
tips: Use job titles with a or an when you mention one person.
Present simple describes regular tasks at work.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Ken works in a hotel and answers guest questions.
choices: He works at the front desk
He drives a train every Sunday
acceptableAnswers: He works at the front desk
exercises.hint: Pick the workplace description that fits a hotel job.
exercises.explanation: He works at the front desk fits someone who helps hotel guests.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: My mother ___ emails and makes reports at the office.
exercises.hint: Use the present simple verb with my mother.
exercises.explanation: Writes agrees with the singular subject my mother.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: Sara works in a small design studio
exercises.hint: Put the workplace phrase after the verb.
exercises.explanation: Sara works in a small design studio is the natural sentence order.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: He is a nurse at the city hospital
exercises.hint: This combines a job and workplace clearly.
exercises.explanation: He is a nurse at the city hospital is a correct description of a job.

lessons.title: Right Now
lessons.subtitle: Present continuous
lessons.goal: Describe actions happening now with present continuous.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Describe actions happening now with present continuous.
tips: Use be plus verb ing for actions happening now.
Look or right now often signal present continuous.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Look at Luis. He has a phone in his hand and he is smiling at the screen.
choices: He reads a message every Monday
exercises.hint: The action is happening now.
exercises.explanation: He is reading a message matches the present moment in the prompt.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: We are ___ for the teacher in the hallway.
exercises.hint: Use the ing form after are.
exercises.explanation: Waiting completes the present continuous structure are waiting.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: My friends are studying for the test
exercises.hint: Keep are before studying.
exercises.explanation: My friends are studying for the test is the correct present continuous order.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: I am cook dinner now
exercises.hint: Check the verb form after am.
exercises.explanation: This sentence is not correct. Say I am cooking dinner now.

lessons.title: Rules and Responsibilities
lessons.subtitle: Have to and do not have to
lessons.goal: Talk about obligations and simple rules.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Talk about obligations and simple rules.
tips: Use have to for something necessary.
Use do not have to when something is not necessary.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Employees must wear an ID card at all times.
choices: They have to wear an ID card
They do not have to wear an ID card
acceptableAnswers: They have to wear an ID card
exercises.hint: Think about obligation.
exercises.explanation: They have to wear an ID card matches the meaning of must here.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: Students do not have to ___ uniforms on Friday.
exercises.hint: Use the base verb after have to.
exercises.explanation: Wear is the correct base verb after do not have to.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: I have to finish this report today
exercises.hint: Keep have to together before the main verb.
exercises.explanation: I have to finish this report today uses the correct obligation pattern.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: You have to be here by eight
exercises.hint: This expresses a rule or requirement.
exercises.explanation: You have to be here by eight is a correct sentence about obligation.

units.title: Plans and Schedules
units.focus: Making plans, routines, invitations, and time expressions for future events

lessons.title: After Work Plans
lessons.subtitle: Evening activities
lessons.goal: Talk about simple after-work plans and suggestions.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Talk about simple after-work plans and suggestions.
tips: Use let us for suggestions you share with another person.
Use after plus a noun phrase to place an event in time.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best reply.
exercises.promptDetail: A: Let us get dinner after work. B: ____
exercises.hint: Reply to the suggestion directly.
exercises.explanation: That sounds good is a natural positive reply to a suggestion.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: We can meet ___ the office at six fifteen.
exercises.hint: Use the place word for the area near the building.
exercises.explanation: Outside completes the meeting plan naturally.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: I am free after five today
exercises.hint: Keep after five together.
exercises.explanation: I am free after five today is a clear sentence about availability.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: How about dinner near the station
exercises.hint: This is a useful suggestion sentence.
exercises.explanation: How about dinner near the station is a correct and natural suggestion.

lessons.title: Weekly Schedule
lessons.subtitle: Times, days, and frequency
lessons.goal: Read and describe a simple weekly schedule.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Read and describe a simple weekly schedule.
tips: Use on for days and at for exact times.
Twice a week describes repeated events clearly.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: The team meeting is on Monday and Thursday at nine.
choices: The team meets twice a week
The team was at the beach
acceptableAnswers: The team meets twice a week
exercises.hint: Count how often the meeting happens.
exercises.explanation: The team meets twice a week correctly summarizes the schedule.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: Our lesson starts ___ Wednesday morning.
exercises.hint: Use the preposition for days.
exercises.explanation: On is the correct preposition before Wednesday morning.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: She usually has dance class on Friday
exercises.hint: Put usually before the main verb.
exercises.explanation: She usually has dance class on Friday uses natural adverb placement.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: The class is every Tuesday at seven
exercises.hint: This is a correct schedule sentence.
exercises.explanation: The class is every Tuesday at seven is a clear way to give a schedule.

lessons.title: This Weekend
lessons.subtitle: Near-future plans
lessons.goal: Talk about simple plans for this weekend.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Talk about simple plans for this weekend.
tips: This weekend often works with present continuous or going to.
A time phrase helps turn a wish into a real plan.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Mina plans to visit her cousin on Saturday afternoon.
choices: She is going to visit her cousin on Saturday afternoon
She visited her cousin on Saturday afternoon
She visits her cousin every year yesterday
acceptableAnswers: She is going to visit her cousin on Saturday afternoon
exercises.hint: Choose the future plan sentence.
exercises.explanation: She is going to visit her cousin on Saturday afternoon correctly describes a plan.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: I am ___ my room this weekend because it is messy.
exercises.hint: Use present continuous for an arranged near-future plan.
exercises.explanation: Cleaning works here because the plan is already arranged for this weekend.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: We are meeting our friends on Sunday
exercises.hint: Keep are meeting together.
exercises.explanation: We are meeting our friends on Sunday is a natural near-future arrangement sentence.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: They are going to stay home tonight
exercises.hint: This sentence talks about a plan.
exercises.explanation: They are going to stay home tonight is a correct future plan sentence.

lessons.title: Changing Plans
lessons.subtitle: Rescheduling and apologies
lessons.goal: Explain a change in plans and suggest another time.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Explain a change in plans and suggest another time.
tips: Use cannot or cannot make it to explain a problem.
Another time keeps the conversation polite and flexible.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best reply.
exercises.promptDetail: A: Can you come at six? B: ____
choices: Sorry, I cannot make it at six
acceptableAnswers: Sorry, I cannot make it at six
exercises.hint: Choose the sentence that politely changes the plan.
exercises.explanation: Sorry, I cannot make it at six clearly explains the schedule problem.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: Can we meet ___ instead?
exercises.hint: Use the time word that suggests a later option.
exercises.explanation: Later completes the rescheduling question naturally.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: I have to work late tonight
exercises.hint: Keep have to before the verb.
exercises.explanation: I have to work late tonight is a clear reason for changing plans.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: How about tomorrow morning
exercises.hint: This is a natural way to suggest a new time.
exercises.explanation: How about tomorrow morning is a correct and useful rescheduling suggestion.

units.title: Looking Back
units.focus: Past simple regular and irregular verbs, past time markers, then and now

lessons.title: Yesterday Morning
lessons.subtitle: Past simple regular verbs
lessons.goal: Describe completed actions yesterday with regular past forms.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Describe completed actions yesterday with regular past forms.
tips: Past time words such as yesterday make the time clear.
Regular verbs often add ed in the past.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Yesterday, Hana cleaned her room and studied English before lunch.
choices: She cleaned her room and studied English
She cleans her room and studies English now
She is clean her room yesterday
acceptableAnswers: She cleaned her room and studied English
exercises.hint: Choose the sentence in the past.
exercises.explanation: She cleaned her room and studied English correctly reports two past actions.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: I ___ my mother after breakfast yesterday.
exercises.hint: Use the past form of call.
exercises.explanation: Called is the correct past simple form here.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: We watched a funny video last night
exercises.hint: Put the past time phrase at the end.
exercises.explanation: We watched a funny video last night follows natural past simple order.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: He cook dinner yesterday
exercises.hint: Check the past form of cook.
exercises.explanation: This sentence is not correct. Say He cooked dinner yesterday.

lessons.title: Last Weekend
lessons.subtitle: Past simple irregular verbs
lessons.goal: Use common irregular past verbs to talk about last weekend.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Use common irregular past verbs to talk about last weekend.
tips: Irregular verbs change form, so memorize common ones such as go and buy.
Last weekend is a useful past time phrase.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: On Saturday, Omar went to the beach and bought a hat.
choices: He went to the beach and bought a hat
He goes to the beach and buys a hat yesterday
He is go to the beach
acceptableAnswers: He went to the beach and bought a hat
exercises.hint: Choose the sentence with the correct irregular past forms.
exercises.explanation: He went to the beach and bought a hat uses the correct past forms of go and buy.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: My friends ___ a great cafe near the station last weekend.
exercises.hint: Use the past form of find.
exercises.explanation: Found is the correct irregular past form of find.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: She had a quiet Sunday at home
exercises.hint: Keep had before the noun phrase.
exercises.explanation: She had a quiet Sunday at home is a clear past simple sentence.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: We saw our teacher at the museum
exercises.hint: Saw is the past form of see.
exercises.explanation: We saw our teacher at the museum is a correct past simple sentence.

lessons.title: A Busy Trip
lessons.subtitle: Past events in sequence
lessons.goal: Narrate a short trip with past time expressions and linked events.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Narrate a short trip with past time expressions and linked events.
tips: Use because or so only when the sequence is clear.
Arrived, missed, and took are common travel verbs in the past.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: The train was late, so Yuki arrived at the hotel at ten.
choices: She arrived late at the hotel
She is arriving at the hotel tomorrow
acceptableAnswers: She arrived late at the hotel
exercises.hint: Choose the sentence that matches the past event.
exercises.explanation: She arrived late at the hotel matches the information in the prompt.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: They ___ the bus because they left the house too late.
exercises.hint: Use the past form of miss.
exercises.explanation: Missed completes the past action correctly.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: I took many photos during the trip
exercises.hint: Put took right after the subject.
exercises.explanation: I took many photos during the trip is the natural word order.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: After dinner, we walked by the river
exercises.hint: This clearly describes a past event.
exercises.explanation: After dinner, we walked by the river is a correct sentence about the past.

lessons.title: Then and Now
lessons.subtitle: Past and present comparison
lessons.goal: Compare past situations with present ones in simple ways.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Compare past situations with present ones in simple ways.
tips: Use lived for a past home and lives for a current home.
Past and present can sit in the same sentence if the time is clear.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: When Ken was ten, he lived in Sapporo. Now he lives in Tokyo.
choices: He is living in Sapporo at ten
exercises.hint: Notice the change between before and now.
exercises.explanation: He lived in Sapporo before correctly describes the earlier situation.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: My grandmother ___ in Osaka when she was a child.
exercises.hint: Use the past form because the time is finished.
exercises.explanation: Lived is the correct past simple verb here.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: He played soccer as a child
exercises.hint: Put the time phrase at the end.
exercises.explanation: He played soccer as a child is a correct sentence about the past.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: I was shy in school, but I am more confident now
exercises.hint: This compares a past state with a present state.
exercises.explanation: I was shy in school, but I am more confident now correctly compares then and now.

units.title: Travel and Transport
units.focus: Stations, hotels, future travel plans, asking for help on the road

lessons.title: At the Station
lessons.subtitle: Tickets and platforms
lessons.goal: Ask simple questions about trains, tickets, and departure information.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Ask simple questions about trains, tickets, and departure information.
tips: Use to with a destination.
Platform, return ticket, and leaves are common station words.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best question.
exercises.promptDetail: You want to know the departure platform.
choices: Which platform does the train leave from
acceptableAnswers: Which platform does the train leave from
exercises.hint: You need the question about train location.
exercises.explanation: Which platform does the train leave from is the correct question for platform information.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: I need a ticket ___ Kyoto, please.
exercises.hint: Use the preposition for destination.
exercises.explanation: To is the standard preposition before a destination.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: The next train leaves in ten minutes
exercises.hint: Keep in ten minutes together as a time phrase.
exercises.explanation: The next train leaves in ten minutes is the natural order for travel information.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: Return ticket means a ticket to go and come back
exercises.hint: Think about two directions in one trip.
exercises.explanation: Return ticket means travel out and back, so the statement is correct.

lessons.title: Booking a Room
lessons.subtitle: Hotel requests
lessons.goal: Make simple hotel requests and ask about room details.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Make simple hotel requests and ask about room details.
tips: Use would like for polite hotel requests.
Use have to ask if a room includes something.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: You want a quiet room for two nights.
choices: I would like a quiet room for two nights
I am quiet two nights room
acceptableAnswers: I would like a quiet room for two nights
exercises.hint: Choose the full polite request.
exercises.explanation: I would like a quiet room for two nights is a clear and polite hotel request.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: Does the room ___ breakfast included?
exercises.hint: Use the verb that asks about included features.
exercises.explanation: Have fits naturally in this question about room features.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: Can we check in after three
exercises.hint: Start with the question form.
exercises.explanation: Can we check in after three is a normal hotel question.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: I need two towels, please
exercises.hint: This is a normal hotel request.
exercises.explanation: I need two towels, please is a clear and polite request for hotel service.

lessons.title: Travel Plans
lessons.subtitle: Going to for future plans
lessons.goal: Talk about planned future travel with going to.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Talk about planned future travel with going to.
tips: Use be going to plus a base verb for plans.
Time phrases such as next month help show future meaning.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Next month, Sara plans to visit her aunt in Fukuoka.
choices: She is going to visit her aunt next month
She visited her aunt next month
acceptableAnswers: She is going to visit her aunt next month
exercises.hint: Choose the future plan sentence.
exercises.explanation: She is going to visit her aunt next month correctly expresses a plan.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: We are going to ___ the museum after lunch.
exercises.hint: Use the base verb after going to.
exercises.explanation: Visit is the correct base verb after are going to.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: I am going to pack my bag tonight
exercises.hint: Keep am going to together before the verb.
exercises.explanation: I am going to pack my bag tonight is the correct future plan pattern.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: They are going to take the early bus
exercises.hint: This describes a planned action.
exercises.explanation: They are going to take the early bus is a correct sentence about a future plan.

lessons.title: Asking for Help on the Road
lessons.subtitle: Practical travel questions
lessons.goal: Ask simple travel questions when you are lost or need help.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Ask simple travel questions when you are lost or need help.
tips: Excuse me is a useful opener with strangers.
Show me on the map is a practical travel question.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best question.
exercises.promptDetail: You are lost and want to find city hall.
choices: Excuse me, how can I get to city hall
acceptableAnswers: Excuse me, how can I get to city hall
exercises.hint: Choose the polite travel question.
exercises.explanation: Excuse me, how can I get to city hall is the natural question when you are lost.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: Could you show me ___ the map, please?
exercises.hint: Use the preposition in the phrase show me on the map.
exercises.explanation: On completes the phrase show me on the map.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: Is there a taxi stand near here
exercises.hint: Start with Is there.
exercises.explanation: Is there a taxi stand near here is a correct and useful travel question.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: I am looking for the bus terminal
exercises.hint: This clearly explains what you need.
exercises.explanation: I am looking for the bus terminal is a correct sentence for asking help on the road.

units.title: Health and Body
units.focus: Symptoms, pharmacy language, healthy habits, and simple advice

lessons.title: Not Feeling Well
lessons.subtitle: Basic symptoms
lessons.goal: Talk about common health problems and body pain.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Talk about common health problems and body pain.
tips: Use have with symptoms such as a headache or a fever.
Use hurts to describe a painful body part.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Mina has a sore throat and a fever.
choices: She is late for breakfast every city
exercises.hint: Choose the sentence that matches the symptoms.
exercises.explanation: She does not feel well is the natural summary of these symptoms.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: I have a headache, so I need to ___ down.
exercises.hint: Use the verb for resting on a bed or sofa.
exercises.explanation: Lie completes the sentence naturally when someone has a headache.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: He has a bad cough today
exercises.hint: Put has after the subject.
exercises.explanation: He has a bad cough today is the correct symptom sentence.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: My arm hurts
exercises.hint: This is a normal way to describe pain.
exercises.explanation: My arm hurts is a correct sentence for body pain.

lessons.title: At the Pharmacy
lessons.subtitle: Medicine and instructions
lessons.goal: Ask for medicine and understand simple usage instructions.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Ask for medicine and understand simple usage instructions.
tips: Anything for a cold is a common pharmacy request.
Take after meals is a common medicine instruction.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best question.
exercises.promptDetail: You want medicine for a cold.
choices: Do you have anything for a cold
acceptableAnswers: Do you have anything for a cold
exercises.hint: Choose the useful pharmacy request.
exercises.explanation: Do you have anything for a cold is a natural way to ask for medicine.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: Take these tablets ___ meals.
exercises.hint: Use the common instruction phrase.
exercises.explanation: After completes the phrase after meals.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: This medicine can make you sleepy
exercises.hint: Keep can before the base verb make.
exercises.explanation: This medicine can make you sleepy is the natural word order.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: Use this cream twice a day
exercises.hint: This gives a medicine instruction.
exercises.explanation: Use this cream twice a day is a correct sentence for medicine directions.

lessons.title: Healthy Habits
lessons.subtitle: Routine health advice
lessons.goal: Talk about healthy routines and simple advice.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Talk about healthy routines and simple advice.
tips: Use should for advice, not a strong rule.
Habit sentences often use present simple with time phrases.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Omar walks to work, drinks water, and sleeps eight hours.
choices: He was at a hotel yesterday
exercises.hint: Choose the general idea that matches the routine.
exercises.explanation: He has healthy habits is the best summary of the routine.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: You should ___ more water during the day.
exercises.hint: Use the base verb after should.
exercises.explanation: Drink is the correct base verb after should.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: She goes to bed early on weekdays
exercises.hint: Keep the time phrase at the end.
exercises.explanation: She goes to bed early on weekdays is a natural routine sentence.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: You should to exercise every day
exercises.hint: Check the form after should.
exercises.explanation: This sentence is not correct. Say You should exercise every day.

lessons.title: Doctor's Advice
lessons.subtitle: Simple treatment suggestions
lessons.goal: Understand and give simple advice after a health problem.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Understand and give simple advice after a health problem.
tips: Use should and should not for calm advice.
See a doctor is a common next step for stronger problems.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best reply.
exercises.promptDetail: A: I have a backache. What should I do? B: ____
choices: You should rest and stretch gently
acceptableAnswers: You should rest and stretch gently
exercises.hint: Reply with advice.
exercises.explanation: You should rest and stretch gently is the appropriate advice in this context.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: You should not ___ heavy bags for a few days.
exercises.hint: Use the base verb after should not.
exercises.explanation: Carry is the correct base verb after should not.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: He should see a doctor soon
exercises.hint: Keep should before the base verb.
exercises.explanation: He should see a doctor soon is the natural advice pattern.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: You should eat something before the medicine
exercises.hint: This gives advice about medicine use.
exercises.explanation: You should eat something before the medicine is a correct advice sentence.

units.title: Weather and Comparing
units.focus: Weather, seasons, comparatives, superlatives, and simple reasons

lessons.title: Today's Weather
lessons.subtitle: Current weather language
lessons.goal: Describe weather conditions and short weather plans.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Describe weather conditions and short weather plans.
tips: Weather sentences often use it is.
Going to rain is a common weather forecast pattern.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: It is cold, gray, and rainy in Osaka this morning.
exercises.hint: Choose the weather description that matches.
exercises.explanation: It is rainy and cold correctly summarizes the weather in the prompt.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: Do not forget your umbrella because it is going to ___.
exercises.hint: Use the base verb after going to.
exercises.explanation: Rain completes the forecast naturally.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: The wind is strong near the coast
exercises.hint: Put the place phrase at the end.
exercises.explanation: The wind is strong near the coast is the natural word order.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: It is twenty degrees and cloudy
exercises.hint: This is a standard weather sentence.
exercises.explanation: It is twenty degrees and cloudy is a correct way to report the weather.

lessons.title: Seasons and Activities
lessons.subtitle: Seasonal habits
lessons.goal: Connect seasons, weather, and typical activities.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Connect seasons, weather, and typical activities.
tips: Use in with seasons such as in spring.
Favorite season is a common way to express preference.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: In winter, Yuki likes hot soup and warm sweaters.
choices: She goes swimming in the snow
exercises.hint: Choose the sentence that matches the season habits.
exercises.explanation: She likes winter things is the best summary of the prompt.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: We usually go hiking ___ spring when the air is cool.
exercises.hint: Use the normal preposition with seasons.
exercises.explanation: In is the standard preposition before spring.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
exercises.hint: Put is after the subject.
exercises.explanation: Autumn is my favorite season is the natural order for a preference sentence.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: People often visit the beach in summer
exercises.hint: This is a normal general statement about a season.
exercises.explanation: People often visit the beach in summer is a correct seasonal statement.

lessons.title: Comparing Cities
lessons.subtitle: Comparatives
lessons.goal: Use comparative forms to compare places and daily life.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Use comparative forms to compare places and daily life.
tips: Add er to many short adjectives.
Use more with longer adjectives when needed.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Tokyo has more people than Sendai.
exercises.hint: Choose the sentence that shows comparison.
exercises.explanation: Tokyo is larger than Sendai matches the idea of a bigger city.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: This street is ___ than the main road, so it is safer for children.
exercises.hint: Use the comparative of narrow.
exercises.explanation: Narrower is the correct comparative form in this sentence.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: My town is busier than before
exercises.hint: Keep busier before than before.
exercises.explanation: My town is busier than before is the correct comparative structure.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: Kyoto is more old than Tokyo
exercises.hint: Old has an irregular comparative.
exercises.explanation: This sentence is not correct. Say Kyoto is older than Tokyo.

lessons.title: The Best Choice
lessons.subtitle: Superlatives and simple reasons
lessons.goal: Use superlatives to choose the best option in everyday situations.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Use superlatives to choose the best option in everyday situations.
tips: Use the before a superlative.
Best, cheapest, and most comfortable help make decisions.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Of all the cafes in the area, Blue Leaf has the cheapest lunch.
choices: Blue Leaf is the cheapest cafe for lunch
Blue Leaf is cheaper than every train
Blue Leaf cheapest lunch is are
acceptableAnswers: Blue Leaf is the cheapest cafe for lunch
exercises.hint: Choose the full superlative sentence.
exercises.explanation: Blue Leaf is the cheapest cafe for lunch correctly uses a superlative for comparison among many cafes.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: Today is the ___ day of the week for me because I have no classes.
exercises.hint: Use the superlative form of good.
exercises.explanation: Best is the correct superlative form here.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: This is the most comfortable chair here
exercises.hint: Keep the superlative phrase together.
exercises.explanation: This is the most comfortable chair here is the correct superlative pattern.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: Mount Fuji is one of the most famous places in Japan
exercises.hint: This uses a superlative idea with one of the most.
exercises.explanation: Mount Fuji is one of the most famous places in Japan is a correct superlative sentence.

units.title: Experiences and Communication
units.focus: Present perfect for experience, phone messages, and explaining problems

lessons.title: Have You Ever Tried It?
lessons.subtitle: Present perfect with ever and never
lessons.goal: Ask and answer simple questions about life experience.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Ask and answer simple questions about life experience.
tips: Use have you ever for experience questions.
Use the past participle after have.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best question.
exercises.promptDetail: You want to ask about a new food experience.
choices: Have you ever tried Indian curry
Did you ever tried Indian curry
Are you ever try Indian curry
acceptableAnswers: Have you ever tried Indian curry
exercises.hint: Choose the present perfect question.
exercises.explanation: Have you ever tried Indian curry is the correct pattern for an experience question.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: I have never ___ sushi with avocado before.
exercises.hint: Use the past participle of eat.
exercises.explanation: Eaten is the correct past participle after have.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
exercises.hint: Keep has before the participle.
exercises.explanation: She has visited Seoul twice is the correct present perfect order.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: Have you ever ridden a horse
exercises.hint: This asks about life experience.
exercises.explanation: Have you ever ridden a horse is a correct experience question.

lessons.title: Life Experiences
lessons.subtitle: Already, never, and long experiences
lessons.goal: Talk about completed experiences that connect to the present.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Talk about completed experiences that connect to the present.
tips: Use already for something completed sooner than expected.
Use for with a length of time such as for twenty years.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: Ken has been to Singapore two times in his life.
exercises.hint: Choose the sentence about life experience, not a specific day.
exercises.explanation: He has visited Singapore twice correctly expresses experience without a specific time.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: My parents have ___ in the same town for twenty years.
exercises.hint: Use the past participle of live.
exercises.explanation: Lived is the correct participle in this present perfect sentence.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: We have never seen that movie
exercises.hint: Place never after have.
exercises.explanation: We have never seen that movie uses natural present perfect word order.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: I have finished my homework already
exercises.hint: This connects a completed action to now.
exercises.explanation: I have finished my homework already is a correct present perfect sentence.

lessons.title: Leaving a Message
lessons.subtitle: Phone messages and requests
lessons.goal: Leave a simple phone message and report basic information.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Leave a simple phone message and report basic information.
tips: Call me back is a very common phone phrase.
Use left a message for a completed phone action.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best message.
exercises.promptDetail: Your friend is not home. You want him to call you tonight.
choices: Please tell him to call me tonight
I am calling yesterday at home
acceptableAnswers: Please tell him to call me tonight
exercises.hint: Choose the message someone can pass on.
exercises.explanation: Please tell him to call me tonight is a clear message for another person to pass on.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: Can you ask Aya to ___ me back after dinner?
exercises.hint: Use the common phone verb in call me back.
exercises.explanation: Call completes the fixed phrase call me back.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: I left a message for my manager
exercises.hint: Keep left right after the subject.
exercises.explanation: I left a message for my manager is the natural past sentence for phone communication.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: She said she was late for the meeting
exercises.hint: This reports a message from another person.
exercises.explanation: She said she was late for the meeting correctly reports the content of a message.

lessons.title: Explaining Problems
lessons.subtitle: Because and so
lessons.goal: Explain simple problems, causes, and results.
lessons.narrative: Use this lesson in a short everyday scene. Explain simple problems, causes, and results.
tips: Use because before the reason.
Use so before the result.

exercises.prompt: Choose the best sentence.
exercises.promptDetail: The train was delayed, so Emi arrived late.
choices: She arrived late because the train was delayed
She delayed the train because late
She is delay at the restaurant
acceptableAnswers: She arrived late because the train was delayed
exercises.hint: Choose the sentence that keeps the same cause and result.
exercises.explanation: She arrived late because the train was delayed expresses the same meaning clearly.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank.
exercises.promptDetail: I forgot my charger, so my phone is ___.
exercises.hint: Use the everyday adjective for no battery.
exercises.explanation: Dead is a common everyday word for a phone with no battery.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the correct order.
acceptableAnswers: We stayed home because it was stormy
exercises.hint: Put the result first, then the reason.
exercises.explanation: We stayed home because it was stormy is the natural order for this cause-and-result sentence.

exercises.prompt: Decide if the sentence or idea is correct.
exercises.promptDetail: I was tired, so I went to bed early
exercises.hint: This clearly shows cause and result.
exercises.explanation: I was tired, so I went to bed early is a correct sentence using so.
